response_json.brief_summary: Keep hq.html as a cornerstone progression guide, but tighten the first-screen answer so it explicitly cross-checks HQ requirements and dependency planning without changing page role or cluster fit.
response_json.primary_user_job: Verify HQ requirement planning, construction dependencies, and progression-route coverage against reference data.
response_json.first_screen_plan: Preserve the answer-first structure. Keep the opening focused on the best HQ path for most players, but make the first visible answer clearly state the requirement cross-check angle: early rush, required-buildings-only upgrades, HQ30 target, then a separate HQ31-35 steel phase. Avoid adding a new intent or expanding into a different guide type. Keep the short supporting line immediately after the answer so users can confirm this is a planning guide, not a full route map.

section_plan.action: Keep the existing answer-first block, but make the promise more explicit about HQ requirement verification and progression planning.
section_plan.reason: This is the highest-value first-screen area and should immediately satisfy the cross-check intent.

section_plan.action: Add or refine the summary logic around when to rush, when to stop for required buildings, and when HQ30 becomes the main checkpoint.
section_plan.reason: Users need a simple decision rule, not just a level list.

section_plan.action: Clarify the difference between normal early progression and the heavier HQ31-35 phase, including why steel changes the plan.
section_plan.reason: This keeps the guide useful for planning and reduces confusion about the late-game jump.

section_plan.action: Keep the route block limited to the existing progression cluster and make the internal route order explicit.
section_plan.reason: The page should reinforce the existing cluster role and avoid drifting into unrelated topics.

section_plan.action: Retain related links, but prioritize pages that support progression, leveling, building order, and resource planning.
section_plan.reason: This helps users move to adjacent planning pages without broadening the page intent.

internal_link_plan.reason: Keep the main hub link as the top-level route into the progression cluster.
Supports new players who need the earliest progression context before HQ planning.
Helps users optimize the pre-HQ30 phase without duplicating HQ-specific coverage.
Directly supports dependency and building-priority planning.
Useful follow-up for users who want detailed HQ cost and requirement data.
Supports resource planning around upgrade timing and bottlenecks.
Relevant for the HQ31-35 progression phase where steel becomes the limiter.

do_not_change: Do not change the existing page template, navigation pattern, or schema family.
Do not turn hq.html into a different page role or a new intent.
Do not add unsupported external game claims beyond the current canonical framing.
Do not use analytics as proof of a rewrite need.
Do not broaden the page into a competitor-style reference dump.
Do not create a new page for this opportunity.
owner_questions: Can the external HQ and progression reference be confirmed against canonical site memory or owner-approved reference data before any user-visible wording is finalized?
Do you want the first-screen answer to mention HQ30 and HQ31-35 explicitly, or keep the current phrasing and only sharpen the supporting sentence?
Should the cluster route block stay exactly as-is structurally, with only wording tightened for route clarity?
acceptance_checks: manual review: first-screen answer and internal links on hq.html

exact_replacements.exact_old: Best Last Z HQ upgrade path for most players: rush early levels, upgrade only required buildings, push efficiently to HQ30 first, and treat HQ31-35 as a separate steel-based progression phase with much heavier timers.
exact_replacements.exact_new: Best Last Z HQ upgrade path for most players: rush early levels, upgrade only the buildings required for the next HQ checkpoint, push efficiently to HQ30 first, and treat HQ31-35 as a separate steel-based progression phase with much heavier timers.
exact_replacements.reason: This is a narrow first-screen refinement that preserves the current structure while improving requirement clarity.

response_json.next_step: Have an owner verify the external reference against canonical knowledge, then decide whether this can be applied as a narrow first-screen clarification with no page-role change.

safety: No content, backlog, manifest, or production files were modified by the LLM adapter.
